title: ResMed Astral Ventilator - Power or Battery Failure
description: Guide for troubleshooting power or battery failures on a ResMed Astral ventilator, focusing on external checks before internal repair.

steps.title: Verify AC Power Connection
steps.instructions: Ensure the ventilator is properly plugged into a live, grounded outlet. Check for any visible damage to the AC power cord. Confirm the outlet is working by testing another device. Why: A faulty outlet or loose connection is a common cause of power failure.

steps.title: Check Battery Installation
steps.instructions: Ensure the battery is fully seated in its compartment. Look for corrosion, debris, or damage on the battery contacts. Why: Poor battery contact can prevent charging or power delivery.

steps.title: Test with a Known Good Battery
steps.instructions: Swap in a verified working battery if available. Confirm if the device powers on or charges normally. Why: This isolates whether the battery itself is the issue.

steps.title: Inspect Battery Charging
steps.instructions: Confirm the device indicates charging when plugged in. Allow several minutes to see if charging indicators respond. Why: Charging failure may suggest battery end-of-life or power supply issues.

steps.title: Check Device Settings and Logs
steps.instructions: Review any error messages displayed on the ventilator. Record any battery or power-related alarms. Why: Device logs may indicate if the issue is hardware or software related.

steps.title: Verify DC Power (if applicable)
steps.instructions: If using an external DC source (e.g., vehicle power), ensure voltage matches specifications. Inspect cables and connectors for damage. Why: Incorrect voltage or damaged cables can prevent proper operation.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator will not turn on; battery not charging.
CCR.Cause: Battery contacts clean but device fails to power on with known good battery; AC power verified functional.
CCR.Resolution: Device removed from service, labeled Out of Service, sent to vendor for repair.

helpfulDetails: Device condition: external casing intact, no visible damage.

title: ResMed Astral Ventilator - Alarm Conditions Not Recognized or Not Triggered
description: Troubleshooting guide for situations where a ResMed Astral ventilator fails to recognize or trigger alarm conditions, focusing on external checks before internal failure.

steps.title: Verify Power and Battery Status
steps.instructions: Ensure the ventilator is properly plugged in or the battery is charged. Reason: Some alarm circuits may not function correctly if the device detects low power.

steps.title: Check Ventilator Setup and Mode
steps.instructions: Confirm that the ventilator is in the correct mode for the patient (e.g., CPAP, ST, or PSV) and that settings are applied. Reason: Certain modes may suppress or delay specific alarms if not configured correctly.

steps.title: Inspect Alarm Volume and Settings
steps.instructions: Check the alarm volume and audible alarm settings on the device. Ensure that “Alarm Silence” is not active. Reason: Alarms may not be perceived if the volume is low or muted.

steps.title: Examine Patient Circuit and Connections
steps.instructions: Verify that all tubing, sensors, and patient connections are secure, free from leaks, kinks, or obstructions. Reason: Alarms may not trigger if the device does not detect a patient-related event due to poor circuit integrity.

steps.title: Test Alarm Function Using Built-In Test
steps.instructions: Use the ventilator’s self-test or alarm test function if available. Follow on-screen prompts to simulate alarm conditions. Reason: Confirms whether the alarms can trigger independently of patient factors.

steps.title: Check for External Interference or Environmental Factors
steps.instructions: Ensure there are no high levels of ambient noise or electrical interference that could impact alarm detection. Reason: Environmental factors may cause false suppression or prevent alarm audibility.

CCR.Complaint: Clinician reports that the ventilator alarms do not trigger during disconnection or high-pressure events.
CCR.Cause: Observed that alarm volume was muted and patient circuit had a loose connection. Device passed self-test once connections were secured and volume adjusted.
CCR.Resolution: Reconnected all tubing securely, increased alarm volume, verified alarm function using built-in test. Device returned to service.

helpfulDetails: Battery charged or AC power verified.
Tubing connections checked for leaks or kinks.
Any unusual behavior (e.g., delayed alarm triggering) documented.
Final device status: returned to service or removed for repair.

title: ResMed Astral Ventilator - Circuit Leak or Disconnection Errors
description: Guide for Clinical Engineering to troubleshoot circuit leak or disconnection errors on a ResMed Astral ventilator, focusing on external checks and patient safety.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions:
Move the patient to a backup ventilator if possible.
Confirm oxygen delivery and ventilation are uninterrupted.
Circuit troubleshooting should never occur with an active, unmonitored patient.

steps.title: Check Circuit Connections
steps.instructions:
Inspect all tubing connections from the ventilator to the patient interface (mask, tracheostomy, or endotracheal tube).
Ensure each connector is fully seated and locked.
Loose or misaligned connections are the most common source of leak errors.

steps.title: Inspect Circuit Components
steps.instructions:
Examine the circuit for kinks, pinches, or bends that may restrict airflow.
Check for disconnections at Y-pieces, filters, or humidifier connections.
Replace any damaged tubing or connectors.

steps.title: Check the Patient Interface (Mask/ET Tube)
steps.instructions:
Ensure the mask or ET tube is properly fitted and secured.
Inspect for worn or torn masks or cushions that can create leaks.
Verify that any headgear or straps are properly positioned to maintain a seal.

steps.title: Verify Ventilator Settings
steps.instructions:
Ensure the ventilator’s circuit configuration matches the actual patient circuit (single-limb, dual-limb, with/without humidifier).
Reset alarm thresholds for leaks if previously modified.
Perform a quick system test if available (e.g., leak test or circuit calibration).

steps.title: Check Humidifiers or Water Traps
steps.instructions:
Remove excess condensation or water from the circuit if present.
Ensure heated circuits are correctly installed and not damaged.

steps.title: If the Problem Persists
steps.instructions:
All external connections, tubing, and patient interfaces have been verified.
Internal circuit sensors or ventilator modules may be malfunctioning.
Remove the device from service.
Label the ventilator “Out of Service.”
Send for bench evaluation or repair.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator alarm indicates circuit leak or disconnection; patient pressure readings unstable.
CCR.Cause: Loose tubing connection detected at patient Y-piece; mask cushion slightly torn.
CCR.Resolution: Reconnected all circuit tubing; replaced mask cushion; verified ventilator circuit test passed; monitored patient for stability.

helpfulDetails: Circuit checked for kinks and proper routing
Leak test or circuit calibration performed
Final device status: functioning, alarms cleared

title: ResMed Astral Ventilator - Flow or Tidal Volume Discrepancies
description: Guide for troubleshooting flow or tidal volume discrepancies on a ResMed Astral ventilator, emphasizing external checks and patient safety.

steps.title: Verify Patient Circuit Connection
steps.instructions:
Check that all tubing, humidifiers, filters, and patient interfaces are securely connected.
Loose connections or disconnected circuits can cause inaccurate flow measurements or tidal volume delivery.

steps.title: Inspect Circuit for Leaks
steps.instructions:
Visually inspect the circuit for cracks, holes, or loose fittings.
Use a leak test if the ventilator supports it. Even small leaks can drastically affect tidal volume readings.

steps.title: Check Exhalation Port and Filters
steps.instructions:
Ensure the exhalation port is not obstructed and filters are clean.
Obstruction can cause inaccurate flow sensing and trigger low or high tidal volume alarms.

steps.title: Verify Ventilator Settings vs Patient Requirements
steps.instructions:
Confirm the correct mode (e.g., PS, CPAP, PCV, VC) is selected.
Incorrect mode or improper parameters may appear as a volume discrepancy.

steps.title: Inspect for Water or Condensation in Circuit
steps.instructions:
Remove any pooled water from tubing or humidifiers.
Fluid in the circuit can change flow characteristics, leading to inaccurate tidal volumes.

steps.title: Check for Kinks or Obstructions in Tubing
steps.instructions:
Straighten tubing and ensure there are no sharp bends.
Obstructions alter delivered volumes and trigger alarms.

steps.title: Examine Ventilator Battery or Power Supply
steps.instructions: Ensure stable power; low battery or fluctuating supply can affect flow sensors and measurements.

steps.title: Replace Disposable Components
steps.instructions: Swap patient circuit, filters, and sensors with new units to rule out equipment wear.

CCR.Complaint: Clinician reports tidal volume delivered is inconsistent with set values; low and high tidal volume alarms observed.
CCR.Cause: External inspection revealed circuit and filters intact, no visible leaks; flow discrepancies persist.
CCR.Resolution: Ventilator removed from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent for manufacturer repair. Patient transitioned to backup device.

helpfulDetails: Circuit inspected for leaks, kinks, and condensation
Filters and humidifiers checked or replaced

title: ResMed Astral Ventilator - Humidifier or Accessory Connection Errors
description: Guide for troubleshooting humidifier or accessory connection errors on a ResMed Astral ventilator, emphasizing external checks and accessory verification.

steps.title: Verify device power and readiness
steps.instructions: Ensure the ventilator is powered on and battery or mains supply is stable. A low battery or intermittent power can trigger accessory errors.

steps.title: Inspect accessory connection points
steps.instructions: Check that the humidifier and any connected tubing are fully seated in their ports. Look for misalignment, loose fittings, or obstruction at connectors.

steps.title: Examine accessories for damage
steps.instructions: Inspect the humidifier chamber, tubing, and water chamber for cracks, leaks, or residue. Verify that no water or condensation has entered electrical connection points.

steps.title: Check for correct accessory type
steps.instructions: Confirm that the connected humidifier is compatible with the Astral model. Incompatible devices may cause detection errors.

steps.title: Clean connection contacts
steps.instructions: Power off the ventilator. Wipe electrical contacts on both the ventilator and accessory with a lint-free cloth. Ensure no residue or corrosion is present.

steps.title: Reattach accessory and power cycle
steps.instructions: Reconnect the humidifier or accessory. Power off, wait 10 seconds, and power on to allow device detection routines to run.

steps.title: Swap with known good accessory if available
steps.instructions: If the error persists, connect a different verified humidifier or accessory. This isolates whether the problem is with the device or the accessory.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator displays humidifier connection error; alarm triggered intermittently.
CCR.Cause: Observed humidifier not fully seated; minor residue on connection contacts.
CCR.Resolution: Cleaned contacts, reattached humidifier, powered device off/on; error cleared.

helpfulDetails: No unusual heat, smell, or sounds detected
Final device status: Operating normally with accessory connected

title: ResMed Lumis Ventilator - Power or Battery Failure
description: Guide to troubleshoot power or battery failures on a ResMed Lumis ventilator, focusing on external checks before escalation.

steps.title: Verify AC Power Source
steps.instructions: Ensure the wall outlet is live using a known working device or outlet tester. Check the ventilator power cord for damage, proper connection, and secure seating. Why: A faulty outlet or loose cord is a common cause of “power failure” errors.

steps.title: Check the Ventilator’s Power Switch and Indicators
steps.instructions: Confirm the power switch is in the “ON” position. Observe any indicator lights on the device when connected to AC. Why: Some Lumis models require a full press or hold to power on, and indicator lights signal basic functionality.

steps.title: Inspect the Battery Module
steps.instructions: Remove the battery and inspect for corrosion, physical damage, or improper seating. Reinsert the battery securely until it clicks. Why: A poorly seated battery can prevent charging or device startup.

steps.title: Test with a Known Good Battery
steps.instructions: If available, replace the battery with a fully charged, verified functional battery. Observe if the device powers on or charges. Why: This isolates whether the original battery is the source of failure.

steps.title: Check Charging Behavior
steps.instructions: With AC connected, monitor battery charging indicators. If the battery fails to charge over a reasonable period, note any error messages. Why: Proper charging confirms the internal charging circuit is functioning.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Device
steps.instructions: Disconnect AC power and battery, wait 30–60 seconds, then reconnect AC and battery and attempt to power on. Why: Some electronic errors can be cleared by a simple power reset.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator will not power on; battery not charging.
CCR.Cause: Battery was found loose and corroded; AC power confirmed functional.
CCR.Resolution: Cleaned battery contacts, reseated battery, and verified device powers on and charges. If unresolved, device sent for manufacturer repair.

helpfulDetails: AC outlet tested with a multimeter or known device.
Battery seating checked; swapped with known good battery if available.

title: ResMed Lumis Ventilator - Alarm Not Triggering or Misconfigured
description: Guide to troubleshoot alarm failures or misconfigurations on a ResMed Lumis ventilator, focusing on external checks and patient safety.

steps.title: Verify Device Power
steps.instructions: Ensure the ventilator is connected to a reliable AC source or fully charged battery. Power cycling the device can reset minor configuration glitches. Why: Some alarms may not trigger if the device detects a low-power condition.

steps.title: Check Alarm Volume and Settings
steps.instructions: Access the alarm menu and confirm volume levels are audible. Check that alarm thresholds are set appropriately for the patient. Why: Misconfigured thresholds or muted alarms are a common cause of missed alerts.

steps.title: Inspect Connections and Circuit
steps.instructions: Confirm patient circuit, tubing, and humidifier connections are secure. Ensure the device recognizes all attached accessories. Why: Some alarms depend on detecting a complete circuit or accessory.

steps.title: Review User Profile and Mode Settings
steps.instructions: Ensure the ventilator mode matches the patient therapy requirements. Verify that alarm limits correspond to the selected mode. Why: Switching modes can reset or alter alarm parameters.

steps.title: Perform Self-Test / System Check
steps.instructions: Run the built-in ventilator self-test (if available). Observe for any system alerts or error messages during the test. Why: Self-tests can detect sensor or internal issues that prevent alarm triggering.

steps.title: Check for Software or Firmware Alerts
steps.instructions: Confirm that the device firmware is current. Look for messages indicating the need for calibration or update. Why: Outdated software can cause alarm logic errors.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator alarms not triggering during therapy or appear misconfigured.
CCR.Cause: Alarm settings incorrect, low volume, or device failed self-test.
CCR.Resolution: Adjusted alarm thresholds, verified volume, confirmed circuit connections, ran self-test. Device passed or escalated to repair if still nonfunctional.

helpfulDetails: Alarm volume and threshold settings checked
Final device status (functional or out of service)

title: ResMed Lumis Ventilator - Mask or Circuit Leak Detection Errors
description: Guide to troubleshoot mask or circuit leak detection errors on a ResMed Lumis ventilator with focus on external connections, mask fit, and circuit integrity.

steps.title: Verify Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Ensure the patient is stable and has an alternate ventilator or oxygen support available if necessary. Do not troubleshoot on an active patient without backup support.

steps.title: Check the Mask Fit
steps.instructions: Inspect the mask for proper sizing and fit on the patient. Ensure straps are correctly adjusted, not twisted, and headgear is not overly tight or loose. Small leaks around the mask are a common cause of alarm triggers.

steps.title: Inspect Circuit Connections
steps.instructions: Confirm that all tubing and connectors between the ventilator and mask are securely attached. Check for cracks, holes, or worn areas in the tubing. Reconnect any loose components to ensure airtight connections.

steps.title: Examine Humidifier and Accessory Interfaces
steps.instructions: If using a humidifier or heated circuit, ensure it is properly seated and not introducing leaks. Inspect O-rings or gaskets for integrity.

steps.title: Assess Exhalation Port and Filters
steps.instructions: Make sure exhalation ports are clear and filters are clean. Blocked or dirty ports can cause false leak readings.

steps.title: Run a Device Leak Test
steps.instructions: If the device has a built-in circuit/mask leak test function, perform the test according to prompts. Observe reported leak values; compare to expected baseline for the mask type.

steps.title: Swap Components if Needed
steps.instructions: Replace the mask or circuit with a known-good component to see if the error persists. This isolates whether the issue is with the device or the consumables.

CCR.Complaint: Mask leak alarm keeps triggering during patient use.
CCR.Cause: Observed loose mask straps and minor tubing crack causing intermittent circuit leak.
CCR.Resolution: Adjusted mask fit, replaced damaged tubing, performed leak test confirming normal leak values.

helpfulDetails: Circuit tubing serial numbers or condition
Any humidifier or accessory in use

title: ResMed Lumis Ventilator - Pressure or Tidal Volume Delivery Inconsistencies
description: Guide for troubleshooting inconsistent pressure or tidal volume delivery on a ResMed Lumis ventilator, focusing on external checks before escalation.

steps.title: Verify Power and Battery Status
steps.instructions: Ensure the device is powered on and the battery is sufficiently charged. Low power can cause inconsistent operation. Check that the AC adapter is properly connected if the device is plugged in.

steps.title: Inspect Circuit and Connections
steps.instructions: Examine all tubing, humidifier connections, and masks for kinks, bends, or obstructions. Ensure connectors are fully seated and compatible with the Lumis system. Even minor disconnections can cause pressure or volume inconsistencies.

steps.title: Check for Mask Fit and Leak
steps.instructions: Verify that the mask is properly fitted to the patient. Large leaks can result in inaccurate tidal volume delivery. Swap with a known good mask if issues persist.

steps.title: Evaluate Filters and Humidifier
steps.instructions: Check and replace any clogged or wet filters. Inspect the humidifier chamber for water levels and secure connections. Malfunctioning humidifiers can alter flow resistance.

steps.title: Verify Ventilator Settings
steps.instructions: Confirm that pressure and volume settings match the patient’s prescribed therapy. Look for accidental mode changes or parameter adjustments.

steps.title: Observe Patient Interface and Compliance
steps.instructions: Check for sudden changes in patient effort, airway resistance, or compliance. Document any patient-related factors that could affect delivered volumes.

steps.title: Perform a Quick Functional Test
steps.instructions: Using a test lung or ventilator simulator, verify consistent pressure and tidal volume delivery. If normal on a test lung, the issue may be patient-related rather than device-related.

CCR.Complaint: Patient alarms indicate inconsistent tidal volume and pressure delivery.
CCR.Cause: External circuit leak detected at mask interface; filter partially clogged; settings verified as correct.
CCR.Resolution: Adjusted mask fit, replaced filter, verified proper delivery with test lung. Patient returned to therapy.

helpfulDetails: Final device status: operational or Out of Service

title: ResMed Lumis Ventilator - Humidifier or Tubing Connection Issues
description: Guide for troubleshooting humidifier or tubing connection errors on a ResMed Lumis ventilator with external verification steps.

steps.title: Ensure Safe Patient Environment
steps.instructions: Move the patient to a backup ventilator if needed. Never disconnect tubing or humidifier while patient support is compromised.

steps.title: Verify Device Power and Status
steps.instructions: Check that the Lumis ventilator powers on normally. Confirm battery/AC power status; low power can cause accessory errors.

steps.title: Inspect Humidifier Placement and Connection
steps.instructions: Confirm the humidifier is seated correctly in the base unit. Check for proper alignment of pins, connectors, or magnetic locks. Ensure humidifier water chamber is filled to the recommended level; empty or low water may trigger errors.

steps.title: Examine Tubing and Circuit Connections
steps.instructions: Ensure all tubing connections (inspiratory, expiratory, mask interface) are fully seated. Look for kinks, bends, or obstructions in tubing that may interfere with flow detection. Verify that the correct tubing type is used (manufacturer-specified).

steps.title: Check for Humidifier or Tubing Damage
steps.instructions: Inspect for cracks, leaks, or worn seals. Replace any damaged parts with manufacturer-approved components.

steps.title: Clean Contact Points
steps.instructions: Check connectors for moisture, debris, or condensation. Wipe contacts with a lint-free cloth if needed.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Ventilator
steps.instructions: After connections are verified, restart the ventilator to reset accessory detection. Observe whether the device now recognizes the humidifier and tubing.

steps.title: Verify Alarm Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm that any previously triggered humidifier/circuit alarms are cleared. Document any recurring alarms for further evaluation.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator alarm indicates “humidifier not detected” or circuit disconnected.
CCR.Cause: Humidifier seated incorrectly, tubing loose, or contact points obstructed.
CCR.Resolution: Verified proper humidifier placement, reconnected tubing, cleaned contacts, restarted device; alarms cleared.

helpfulDetails: Confirm AC power and battery status
